patches.content:
Index: 
===================================================================
--- 
+++ 
@@ -1,16 +1,114 @@
+
 FRONTEND INTERNSHIP
 Technical Skills Assessment
 E-Commerce UI — React + Tailwind CSS
+Project	ARCA Store — E-Commerce UI
+Stack	React 18, Tailwind CSS v3, Vite
+Estimated time	4 – 6 hours
+Difficulty	Beginner–Intermediate
+Deliverable	GitHub repo or CodeSandbox link
+Due	To be confirmed by interviewer
 
-Project
-ARCA Store — E-Commerce UI
-Stack
-React 18, Tailwind CSS v3, Vite
-Estimated time
-4 – 6 hours
-Difficulty
-Beginner–Intermediate
-Deliverable
-GitHub repo or CodeSandbox link
-Due
-To be confirmed by interviewer
+1. Overview
+You will build a minimal but polished e-commerce storefront from scratch — entirely based on your own imagination. There is no design file, no Figma link, and no reference UI provided. The product catalogue, colour palette, typography, and overall aesthetic are all your decisions. We want to see how you think, not just whether you can follow instructions.
+The application must function as a complete, self-contained shopping experience across three views: a product listing page, an individual product detail page, and a cart. All styling must be done exclusively with Tailwind CSS utility classes — no custom CSS files, no inline style props, and no component libraries.
+
+2. Functional Requirements
+The following features are mandatory. Each must be fully working and visually complete.
+
+2.1  Products Listing Page
+•Display a grid of product cards — at minimum 6 products, all hardcoded
+•Each card must show: product image, name, category, price, and a rating or short tagline
+•A live search input that filters the visible cards by product name (no submit button — filter on every keystroke)
+•Category filter tabs or buttons to narrow results by product type (e.g. Footwear, Apparel, Accessories)
+•An Add to Cart button on every card that adds the item without navigating away
+•A persistent header or navbar containing a cart icon with a badge showing the total item count
+
+2.2  Product Detail Page
+•Clicking a product card navigates to a full detail view for that product
+•Must include: large product image, name, category, full description, a list of specifications or features, price, and a quantity picker (+/- controls)
+•Add to Cart button that respects the selected quantity
+•If the product is already in the cart, show a secondary button or indicator linking to the cart
+•A back navigation control that returns the user to the listing page without a full page reload
+
+2.3  Cart Page
+•Lists every item currently in the cart with its image, name, price per unit, quantity controls, and line total
+•Each item must have a Remove button that immediately removes it from the cart
+•Quantity can be increased or decreased inline; decreasing to zero removes the item
+•An order summary panel showing: subtotal, shipping cost (free above a threshold of your choice), and grand total
+•A Checkout button (placeholder — no real payment logic required)
+•An empty state message and a link back to products when the cart is empty
+
+2.4  Responsiveness
+•The entire application must be fully responsive using Tailwind breakpoints only
+◦Mobile (< 640 px): single-column layout, stacked navigation, touch-friendly tap targets
+◦Tablet (640 – 1024 px): two-column product grid, adjusted spacing
+◦Desktop (> 1024 px): three-column product grid, side-by-side detail layout, sticky summary panel in cart
+•No horizontal scrollbars at any breakpoint
+
+3. Design Requirements
+This is where we evaluate your taste and judgment. You are designing the store yourself — choose a product category you find interesting and build a visual identity around it.
+
+3.1  Visual Identity
+•Choose a product niche (e.g. minimalist homeware, streetwear, outdoor gear, artisan food, tech accessories — anything)
+•Pick a coherent colour palette of 2 – 4 colours and apply it consistently
+•Use a Google Font or system font stack that feels appropriate for your brand — load it via the index.html <link> tag
+•Maintain consistent spacing, sizing, and corner radius throughout
+
+3.2  UI Polish
+•Hover and focus states on all interactive elements (buttons, cards, inputs)
+•Smooth transitions on hover and state changes using Tailwind transition utilities
+•Images should use Unsplash URLs (free, no key required) — choose images that match your niche
+•Product cards should have a subtle hover effect (scale, shadow lift, or border change)
+•The cart badge must animate or update visibly when an item is added
+
+4. Technical Requirements
+
+4.1  Stack
+•React 18 — bootstrapped with Vite (npm create vite@latest -- --template react)
+•Tailwind CSS v3 — installed via npm, configured in tailwind.config.js
+•No UI component libraries (no shadcn, no MUI, no Chakra, no Ant Design)
+•No CSS Modules, no styled-components, no Sass — Tailwind utility classes only
+•No external state management libraries — useState and prop drilling are sufficient
+
+4.2  Architecture
+•Component breakdown is your decision — but each major UI section should be its own component
+•Cart state must live in the top-level App component and be passed down as props
+•Navigation between pages should use conditional rendering (no React Router required, but you may use it if you prefer)
+•All product data must be a hardcoded JavaScript array — no API calls
+•No TypeScript required, but you may use it if comfortable
+
+4.3  Code Quality
+•Descriptive, consistent component and variable names
+•Props should make sense — avoid passing the entire product object everywhere if only one field is needed
+•No console errors or warnings in the browser during normal use
+•No commented-out dead code in the final submission
+
+5. Bonus (Optional)
+These are not required. Completing them demonstrates initiative and a higher level of polish.
+
+•Skeleton loading state using Tailwind animate-pulse — simulate a 1-second load on mount
+•Dark mode toggle using Tailwind's dark: variant and a class strategy on the <html> element
+•Persist cart to localStorage so it survives a page refresh
+•Smooth page transition when navigating between listing, detail, and cart
+•A toast or notification that briefly appears when an item is added to the cart
+
+6. Evaluation Criteria
+Each submission is assessed across five dimensions. The weight column shows the relative importance of each area.
+
+Criteria	What we look for	Weight
+Functionality	All required features work correctly with no broken states, missing edge cases, or console errors	30%
+Responsiveness	Layout adapts correctly at mobile, tablet, and desktop breakpoints with no overflow or layout breaks	20%
+Component design	Clean separation of concerns, sensible props, no redundant state, readable and maintainable structure	20%
+Tailwind fluency	Correct and idiomatic use of utility classes, responsive prefixes, and state variants — no workarounds	15%
+UI quality	Consistent visual identity, polished hover states, transitions, and spacing that feels intentional	15%
+
+7. Submission Instructions
+
+1.Push your project to a public GitHub repository.
+2.Ensure the project runs with npm install && npm run dev — no additional setup should be required.
+3.Alternatively, deploy it to Vercel or Netlify and share the live URL alongside the repo.
+4.A short README is not required but is appreciated — especially if you made interesting design decisions.
+5.Send the link to your interviewer before the agreed deadline.
+
+Good luck. We look forward to seeing what you build.
\ No newline at end of file
